tests.reason: Small public path-module coverage with no native addon dependency.
Pure JavaScript querystring compatibility surface.
Basic EventEmitter public API coverage.
Representative Buffer allocation and encoding behavior.
Pure JavaScript string_decoder behavior.
WHATWG URLSearchParams public API behavior.
Legacy url.parse query handling.

Requires streaming child_process stdio connected to a separately executing `node --interactive` REPL child. Kandelo's Node compatibility layer currently supports buffered popen-style child output, not interactive child stdin/stdout.

Requires streaming child_process stdio connected to a separately executing `node --interactive` REPL child. Kandelo's Node compatibility layer currently supports buffered popen-style child output, not interactive child stdin/stdout.
Requires Node's --expose-internals support boundary and internal/test/binding native hooks, which the Kandelo compatibility runtime does not expose.
Small console API behavior check.
Tiny timers API smoke case.

The test mixes same-isolate BroadcastChannel behavior with cross-worker delivery. Kandelo models same-isolate BroadcastChannel semantics, but SpiderMonkey shell workers do not share Node's post-start worker_threads message fabric.

The test's final case requires Worker.postMessage() to transfer a MessagePort to an already-running worker. SpiderMonkey shell workers in Kandelo expose eval-time workerData/shared memory only, not post-start worker_threads channels.

The test includes same-isolate MessagePort coverage plus a Worker.postMessage() transfer case. The supported same-isolate API is covered by Kandelo source tests; post-start worker port transfer is an explicit SpiderMonkey shell support boundary.
Requires repeatedly transferring MessagePorts to an already-running worker through Worker.postMessage(), which is outside Kandelo's SpiderMonkey shell worker model.
Requires Node's cross-vm MessagePort wrapper identity from moveMessagePortToContext(). Kandelo validates closed-port errors but does not emulate a separate vm-context port wrapper.
Requires parentPort messaging from a worker plus termination during transfer-list iteration. Kandelo's SpiderMonkey workers do not provide parentPort post-start messaging.
Requires Worker.postMessage() races while terminating workers. Kandelo's SpiderMonkey shell worker support is limited to eval-time workerData/shared memory.
Requires Worker.postMessage() and parentPort replies for SharedArrayBuffer exchange after startup; Kandelo only exposes shared memory through workerData at worker creation.
Requires Worker.postMessage() to send a WebAssembly.Module to an already-running worker, which is outside the SpiderMonkey shell worker support boundary.
Part of the test requires Worker.postMessage() to exchange WebAssembly memory after startup. Same-isolate SharedArrayBuffer MessagePort delivery remains supported separately.
Requires V8 worker heap/resource limit enforcement and ERR_WORKER_OUT_OF_MEMORY. SpiderMonkey shell workers do not expose V8 resource-limit controls.

Requires transferring live MessagePorts and ArrayBuffers through workerData and then communicating over parentPort. Kandelo supports shared-memory workerData, not live MessagePort ownership in workers.
